Yes — I pulled the latest compassionate-use dosing parameters.

**Project Helios – Compassionate Use Dosing Summary**  
**Compound:** NXB-7042  
**Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)  
**Source:** `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`  
**Last updated:** 2026-03-28

| Patient ID | Age | Weight | Dose | Calculated Admin Dose | Frequency | Next Dose | PK Clearance | Status/Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 months | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min | Stable. Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |
| CU-002 | 18 months | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |
| CU-003 | 51 months | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |

**Quick take on PK/model stability:**  
From the dosing config, things look **clinically stable overall**:
- Patient-specific dosing is present and internally consistent.
- PK clearance values are plausible across the three patients.
- Clinical notes are reassuring for CU-001 and CU-002.
- CU-003 is newly enrolled, so that patient likely warrants the closest follow-up.

That said, this file shows the **current dosing parameters**, not live model-health telemetry, so I can’t fully confirm runtime PK model stability from this alone. If you want, I can next check the monitoring/dashboard side for any recent PK-model alerts or drift indicators.
